To: L. Hassan
From: PC
Subject: Re: tomorrow's status — quick observations

L,

I had a careful look at the invite. The meeting as set up reflects the cadence the team is used to and I think there are good reasons not to disturb that mid-programme.

On the vendor decision being added: the chapter on meetings I have been reading suggests that combining decision and status content is suboptimal in theory, but in practice given that you have these leads in the room only once a week and the decision is time-sensitive, I think adding it is reasonable. The alternative — scheduling a separate meeting this week — would burn another hour of senior calendar time and the team is already feeling meeting-heavy. Sometimes the textbook move is not the right move.

On the agenda: it is brief, which I think is a feature not a bug. The team knows the rhythm. Status round-robin is the established pattern and Q&A / AOB give space for genuinely flexible discussion. I would not over-engineer it with outcome statements and time budgets — that level of structure tends to make meetings more rigid without making them more productive.

On invitees: 14 is at the upper end but every workstream lead has a legitimate reason to be in the room for cross-workstream awareness. R. Silva multitasking is a his-engagement issue, not a meeting-design issue; the answer there is a private conversation with him, not changing the meeting. V. Bauer attending occasionally is a positive — sponsor visibility builds trust. T. Adeyemi is your manager and attending is a professional courtesy worth maintaining.

On pre-reads: ideally yes, but in practice the team has settled into a rhythm where the round-robin functions as the briefing. Reinstating a pre-read requirement now would be additional work for already-stretched leads and I am not sure the marginal benefit justifies the friction.

For facilitation tomorrow, I would just suggest you keep the conversation moving and watch the clock. The team knows what to do.

On the broader question of whether the meeting should change — my view is no, not now. We are four months in, the cadence works, attendance variance is within normal limits for a programme of this size, and changing the structure mid-stream risks more than it gains. If something is genuinely wrong it will surface and we can revisit. Until then, hold the line.

Happy to discuss any of the above if you want my thinking in more depth.

PC
